content: The Pharmacy department is located on the Ground Floor, immediately to the right of the main Reception desk. It handles medicine preparation, billing, and dispensing.
The Intensive Care Unit (ICU) is located on the 2nd Floor of Wing B. Elevators near the Cardiology department provide direct access.
The Diagnostic Laboratory is on the 1st Floor of Wing A. Patients can follow the blue signs from the main lobby elevator for blood sample collections and report tracking.
The Emergency Department is on the Ground Floor, with a dedicated entrance on the west side of the campus. It is open 24/7.
The Cafeteria is on the Lower Ground (Basement) Floor, offering fresh meals, beverages, snacks, and seating areas 24/7.
Our general visiting hours are from 10:00 AM - 1:00 PM and 4:00 PM - 7:00 PM daily. ICU visits are strictly limited to immediate family between 5:00 PM - 6:00 PM.
Complimentary multi-level visitor parking is available in Building C. Valet parking is located at the main Emergency Entrance.
Refund requests for cancelled consultations or tests can be initiated at the Billing desk on the Ground Floor. Processing takes 3-5 business days.
We support cashless payments and tie-ups with all major health insurance providers. Please present your insurance card at the TPA desk in the main lobby.
Paracetamol is used to treat mild-to-moderate pain and fever. Take 1 tablet every 4-6 hours as needed. Do not exceed 4000mg (8 tablets) in 24 hours to avoid liver damage.
Amoxicillin is a penicillin-type antibiotic used to treat bacterial infections. Finish the entire prescribed course even if you feel better. Take with food to reduce stomach upset.
Ibuprofen is a nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID) used to reduce pain, swelling, and fever. Always take it with food or milk to prevent stomach irritation.
Atorvastatin is a statin medication used to lower cholesterol. It is usually taken once daily in the evening, with or without food. Avoid excessive grapefruit juice during treatment.
